articles.title: Millionaires tax surges past expectations, once again
articles.description:
The voter-approved surtax has sailed past its $2 billion estimate for another year in a row.

The post Millionaires tax surges past expectations, once again appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Millionaires tax exceeds $2 billion estimate for the second consecutive year.
Tax revenue will support local services and infrastructure improvements in Boston.
Residents can expect enhanced funding for education and public health initiatives.

articles.title: Someone just won a $1M scratch ticket prize in Somerville
articles.description:
The winner plans to buy a summer house.

The post Someone just won a $1M scratch ticket prize in Somerville appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A Somerville resident won a $1 million scratch ticket prize.
The winner plans to purchase a summer house with the winnings.
This win highlights the potential for local economic boost in Somerville.

articles.title: Winthrop man pleads not guilty in wife’s shooting death
articles.description:
Sean Brewer, 58, is charged with manslaughter in the fatal shooting of Jacklyn Berry, 47.

The post Winthrop man pleads not guilty in wife’s shooting death appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Sean Brewer, 58, pleads not guilty to manslaughter in wife Jacklyn Berry's shooting death.
Jacklyn Berry, 47, was fatally shot, raising concerns about domestic violence in Winthrop.
The case highlights ongoing issues of safety and legal proceedings in Boston-area communities.

articles.title: Trillium opens new waterfront beer garden at Boston Harbor Hotel
articles.description:
After leaving the Greenway, the brewery’s seasonal outdoor spot has opened at Boston Harbor Hotel with waterfront views.

The post Trillium opens new waterfront beer garden at Boston Harbor Hotel appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Trillium opens a new waterfront beer garden at Boston Harbor Hotel.
The seasonal outdoor spot features scenic views of the harbor.
Local residents can enjoy a new dining option in the waterfront area.

articles.title: Live in a 1700s house next door to the Salem Witch House
articles.description:
It’s been 80 years since 314 Essex St. was available as a residential property.

The post Live in a 1700s house next door to the Salem Witch House appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: 314 Essex St. is available for residential purchase after 80 years.
The property is located next to the historic Salem Witch House.
This opportunity may attract history enthusiasts and boost local tourism.
